milestones.title: Looks at faces, makes eye contact
milestones.description: Focuses on faces about 8-12 inches away and holds brief eye contact.
milestones.tip: Get down face to face, talk softly, and give plenty of chances to study your face.

milestones.title: Reacts to sounds
milestones.description: Startles at sudden noises and turns toward a familiar voice, especially mom's.
milestones.tip: Talk and sing calmly throughout the day, and try to limit loud, sudden noises.

milestones.title: Calms when held or soothed
milestones.description: Settles down when picked up, gently rocked, or hearing a familiar voice.
milestones.tip: When baby cries, offer skin-to-skin contact, gentle rocking, and a soft voice for comfort.

milestones.title: Social smile
milestones.description: Smiles back at you when you smile or talk to them.
milestones.tip: Smile often and make eye contact, and respond with delight every time they smile back.

milestones.title: Lifts head during tummy time
milestones.description: Can hold their head up for a few seconds while lying on their tummy.
milestones.tip: Do short, supervised tummy time a few times a day while baby is awake and alert.

milestones.title: Coos and makes sounds
milestones.description: Begins making soft vowel sounds like "ahh" and "ooh."
milestones.tip: Echo the sounds back, then pause as if taking turns and wait for a reply.

milestones.title: Follows objects with their eyes
milestones.description: Watches a face or object as it moves slowly in front of them.
milestones.tip: Move a colorful toy slowly side to side about 8 inches from baby's face.

milestones.title: Pushes up on arms during tummy time
milestones.description: Props up on their forearms to lift their head and chest while on their tummy.
milestones.tip: Extend tummy time and place an interesting toy in front to encourage them to look up.

milestones.title: Opens hands and brings them together
milestones.description: Unclenches their fists, brings hands to the middle, and studies their fingers.
milestones.tip: Offer a soft rattle to hold, and gently guide their hands together.

milestones.title: Babbles back when you talk
milestones.description: Makes sounds in response to you, joining in a back-and-forth "conversation."
milestones.tip: Take turns talking: you say something, pause, let them answer, then respond again.

milestones.title: Steady head control
milestones.description: Holds their head steady and balanced when held upright.
milestones.tip: Support them in a sitting position so they can look around and take in the world.

milestones.title: Rolls from back to side
milestones.description: Begins rolling from their back to their side, and eventually onto their tummy.
milestones.tip: Place a toy to one side to encourage rolling, and never leave baby alone on a high surface.

milestones.title: Laughs out loud
milestones.description: Responds to play and silliness with real, out-loud laughter.
milestones.tip: Play peekaboo, give gentle tickles, and make funny sounds to spark giggles.

milestones.title: Reaches for objects
milestones.description: Reaches for a toy they can see and tries to grab it.
milestones.tip: Offer colorful, easy-to-grip toys within reach.

milestones.title: Brings objects to their mouth
milestones.description: Explores things by bringing whatever they grab up to their mouth.
milestones.tip: Offer safe, clean teething toys that aren't small, and keep tiny parts out of reach.

milestones.title: Starts sitting without support
milestones.description: Can sit briefly without support, often propping forward on their hands.
milestones.tip: Surround them with pillows for safe, supported sitting practice.

milestones.title: Babbles syllables (ba-ba, da-da)
milestones.description: Repeats consonant-vowel syllables, stringing them together into babble.
milestones.tip: Echo their syllables and build on them with words, like "Da-da's here!"

milestones.title: Passes objects hand to hand
milestones.description: Can move a toy from one hand to the other.
milestones.tip: Offer a toy to each hand and watch as they practice transferring it.

milestones.title: Recognizes familiar faces
milestones.description: Tells familiar people from strangers and reacts differently to faces they know.
milestones.tip: Give them regular, warm one-on-one time with family members.

milestones.title: Crawls
milestones.description: Scoots on their belly or crawls on hands and knees.
milestones.tip: Make a safe open space on the floor and place a toy just out of reach to encourage movement.

milestones.title: Stands with support
milestones.description: Can stand while holding onto furniture or your hands.
milestones.tip: Let them pull up on low, sturdy furniture, and pad sharp corners in case of falls.

milestones.title: Loves peekaboo
milestones.description: Enjoys hide-and-reveal games and anticipates what comes next.
milestones.tip: Play peekaboo and "where did it go?" often -- it builds object permanence.

milestones.title: Uses a pincer grasp
milestones.description: Picks up small things between their thumb and index finger.
milestones.tip: Offer soft, bite-sized finger foods under supervision, watching closely for choking.

milestones.title: Responds to their name
milestones.description: Turns or reacts when you call their name.
milestones.tip: Call them by name and reward them with a smile when they look.

milestones.title: Shows stranger anxiety
milestones.description: May get shy or cling to you around people they don't know.
milestones.tip: Don't rush introductions with new people -- stay close and offer reassurance.

milestones.title: Pulls up to stand
milestones.description: Pulls themselves up to standing by holding onto furniture.
milestones.tip: Provide sturdy things to grab, and anchor any furniture that could tip over.

milestones.title: Cruises along furniture
milestones.description: Steps sideways while holding onto furniture for support.
milestones.tip: Set up sturdy supports with short gaps between them to encourage cruising.

milestones.title: First steps (with or without support)
milestones.description: Takes first steps while holding your hand or briefly on their own.
milestones.tip: Hold both hands, then one, and cheer them on -- falls are a normal part of learning.

milestones.title: First meaningful word (mama/dada)
milestones.description: Says their first meaningful word, directed at a person or thing.
milestones.tip: Name objects and read books, then repeat and expand on their words.

milestones.title: Waves bye-bye
milestones.description: Waves goodbye and imitates simple gestures.
milestones.tip: Wave every time someone comes or goes, and practice clapping and "bye-bye" together.

milestones.title: Points to what they want
milestones.description: Uses a finger to point at something they want or find interesting.
milestones.tip: Name what they point to ("Yes, a bird!") to build shared attention.

milestones.title: Understands simple words
milestones.description: Understands simple words and gestures like "no" and "come here."
milestones.tip: Give short, clear directions backed up by gestures, and offer praise when they understand.

milestones.title: Drinks from a cup with help
milestones.description: Can take sips from a cup or sippy cup with help.
milestones.tip: Offer a little water in an open or straw cup, and treat spills as part of learning.

milestones.title: Walks well on their own
milestones.description: Walks steadily without support and can bend down to pick something up.
milestones.tip: Let them walk freely in safe spaces and encourage them to explore.

milestones.title: Uses 3-5 meaningful words
milestones.description: Uses a few meaningful words correctly and understands much more.
milestones.tip: Talk, sing, and read together, and expand their words into full sentences.

milestones.title: Scribbles with a crayon
milestones.description: Holds a crayon or marker and begins scribbling on paper.
milestones.tip: Offer chunky crayons and paper, and model scribbling alongside them.

milestones.title: Shows affection
milestones.description: Hugs, kisses, and shows affection toward familiar people.
milestones.tip: Show your love openly and turn hugs and kisses into a back-and-forth game.

milestones.title: Walks independently
milestones.description: Walks comfortably and tries running and walking backward.
milestones.tip: Give them room to move in open spaces and play with a ball or a push toy.

milestones.title: Says several words
milestones.description: Says at least several words (often 10 or more) and understands many more.
milestones.tip: Instead of handing things over right away, wait for and support them in using words.

milestones.title: Follows simple directions
milestones.description: Follows one-step directions like "Give me the ball."
milestones.tip: Invite them to join everyday tasks ("bring your shoe") and praise their success.

milestones.title: Points to body parts
milestones.description: Can point to body parts like their nose, eyes, or feet when asked.
milestones.tip: Play "where's your nose?" in front of a mirror or with a book.

milestones.title: Tries to eat with a spoon
milestones.description: Holds a spoon and tries to feed themselves, even if it gets messy.
milestones.tip: Let them feed themselves and treat the mess as part of learning.

milestones.title: Plays pretend
milestones.description: Does pretend play like feeding a doll or talking on a toy phone.
milestones.tip: Join in pretend play with a toy kitchen or doll and build the story together.

milestones.title: Puts two words together
milestones.description: Combines two words into phrases like "drink water" or "daddy come."
milestones.tip: Model expanding their single word into two ("Water. You want water.").

milestones.title: Runs
milestones.description: Runs with good balance and can stop and change direction.
milestones.tip: Make room for big-muscle play like running and chase at the park.

milestones.title: Climbs stairs holding on
milestones.description: Climbs stairs one step at a time while holding the railing or your hand.
milestones.tip: Let them climb while holding on, under supervision, and practice patiently.

milestones.title: Plays imitation games
milestones.description: Imitates everyday actions like housework or talking on the phone.
milestones.tip: Let them do what you do ("let's sweep together") and play along.

milestones.title: Kicks a ball
milestones.description: Can kick a ball while keeping their balance standing up.
milestones.tip: Play a back-and-forth kicking and rolling game with a soft ball.

milestones.title: Matches colors and shapes
milestones.description: Begins matching the same colors or shapes together.
milestones.tip: Offer shape puzzles and colorful blocks for matching games.

milestones.title: Follows two-step directions
milestones.description: Follows two-step directions like "Pick up your shoe and give it to me."
milestones.tip: Give small two-step tasks during daily routines and praise each success.

milestones.title: Jumps with both feet
milestones.description: Can jump off the ground with both feet at the same time.
milestones.tip: Play jumping games together, like jumping over a line on the floor.

milestones.title: Speaks in 2-3 word sentences
milestones.description: Uses short sentences to share what they want and what they notice.
milestones.tip: Ask open-ended questions ("what are you doing?") and build on their answers.

milestones.title: Turns book pages one at a time
milestones.description: Can turn the pages of a book one at a time.
milestones.tip: Look at picture books together and say, "You turn the next page."

milestones.title: Says their name and age
milestones.description: Can tell you their own name and age when asked.
milestones.tip: Reinforce it in everyday chats by asking their name and age.

milestones.title: Rides a tricycle
milestones.description: Can pedal and ride a tricycle.
milestones.tip: Practice pedaling in a safe space and build the habit of wearing a helmet.

milestones.title: Dresses with help
milestones.description: Tries to put on and take off simple clothes with help.
milestones.tip: Choose easy-to-manage clothes and give them time and chances to dress themselves.

milestones.title: Tries to draw a circle
milestones.description: Tries to copy a circle and simple lines when shown an example.
milestones.tip: Draw together and show them how to make shapes like lines and circles.

milestones.title: Ready for potty training
milestones.description: Notices when they need to go, can let you know, and starts learning to use the potty.
milestones.tip: Build a routine without pressure -- praise successes and stay calm about accidents.
